questions.q: What was Kunti's birth name?
questions.explanation: She was born Pritha and came to be called Kunti after her adoptive father Kuntibhoja.

questions.q: Which sage granted Kunti her god-summoning mantra?
questions.explanation: The irascible sage Durvasa, pleased with her service, taught her the mantra to invoke any god.

questions.q: Who was the firstborn son whom Kunti set adrift on the river?
questions.explanation: As an unwed girl she bore Karna and, fearing disgrace, floated him down the river in a basket.

questions.q: Kunti was the wife of which king?
questions.explanation: Kunti chose Pandu, king of Hastinapura, at her swayamvara and became his first wife.

questions.q: Which god fathered Kunti's first child, Karna?
questions.explanation: Karna was the son of Surya, the sun god, summoned when Kunti first tested the mantra.

questions.q: In whose household was Kunti raised after being given in adoption?
questions.explanation: Her father gave her to King Kuntibhoja, from whom she took the name Kunti.

questions.q: Which three Pandavas were Kunti's own sons?
questions.explanation: Kunti bore Yudhishthira, Bhima, and Arjuna, while the twins were Madri's sons.

questions.q: What relation was Krishna to Kunti?
questions.explanation: Kunti was the sister of Vasudeva, making Krishna her nephew.

questions.q: What power did Durvasa's mantra confer on Kunti?
options: The power to summon any god
The power to cure any illness
The power to change her form
questions.explanation: The mantra let her call any god at will, and each god she summoned granted her a son.

questions.q: Which god fathered Kunti's son Arjuna?
questions.explanation: Arjuna was the son of Indra, king of the gods, the third son born to Kunti and Pandu.

questions.q: Why did the young Kunti first invoke Surya with the mantra?
options: She tested it out of youthful curiosity
Her father demanded proof of the boon
Surya appeared to her in a dream
questions.explanation: Curious whether the mantra truly worked, the maiden Kunti called the rising sun, with fateful results.

questions.q: What marks did Kunti's son Karna bear from birth?
options: A mark of the sun on his brow
questions.explanation: Karna was born with golden armor and earrings fused to his body, gifts of his father Surya.

questions.q: Which unwitting instruction of Kunti's made Draupadi the wife of all five brothers?
options: Bring the alms to me first
Let the eldest claim the prize
Divide the prize with your guru
Share whatever you have brought equally
questions.explanation: Without looking up, Kunti told her sons to share what they had brought, not knowing it was a bride.

questions.q: In Ekachakra, how did Kunti divide the alms her sons collected each night?
options: Equally among all six of them
Half for Bhima and half for the rest
The eldest chose his portion first
She fed guests before her sons
questions.explanation: Knowing Bhima's enormous appetite, Kunti set aside half the alms for him alone.

questions.q: Whom did Kunti send to face the demon Bakasura at Ekachakra?
questions.explanation: Kunti volunteered Bhima to deliver the demon's tribute, repaying their brahmin host's kindness.

questions.q: Before the war, what secret did Kunti finally reveal to Karna?
options: That he was her son and the Pandavas' elder brother
That Duryodhana planned to betray him
That Indra would come begging for his armor
questions.explanation: Kunti went to Karna and revealed his birth, begging him to join his brothers, but he refused to abandon Duryodhana.

questions.q: What promise did Karna give Kunti when she revealed herself as his mother?
options: He would abandon Duryodhana at the final hour
He would never strike the first blow
He would meet her openly after the victory
He would spare all her sons except Arjuna
questions.explanation: Karna vowed to fight only Arjuna, so that whatever happened, Kunti would still have five sons.

questions.q: After the war, what did Kunti's revelation about Karna prompt the grieving Yudhishthira to do?
options: Curse all womankind never to keep a secret
Perform a year of penance for Karna
questions.explanation: Anguished that he had unknowingly killed his brother, Yudhishthira cursed women that no secret would ever stay hidden with them.

questions.q: With whom did Kunti choose to retire to the forest in her final years?
questions.explanation: To her sons' dismay, Kunti walked out of Hastinapura to serve Dhritarashtra and Gandhari in their forest retreat.

questions.q: How did Kunti die?
options: On the northern pilgrimage with her sons
In the forest fire with Dhritarashtra and Gandhari
questions.explanation: Kunti perished in the wildfire that swept the forest retreat, refusing to flee alongside the old king and queen.

questions.q: Where did Kunti stay during her sons' thirteen-year exile?
options: With Kuntibhoja in her childhood home
In a hermitage on the Ganga
questions.explanation: Kunti remained behind in Hastinapura, sheltered in the house of the righteous Vidura.

questions.q: Who restored Kunti's virginity after Karna's birth?
options: The Ganga, through a sacred bath
Surya himself, as he had promised
questions.explanation: Surya granted that her maidenhood remain intact, so the unwed girl was spared lasting disgrace.

questions.q: Who was Kunti's biological father?
questions.explanation: She was born to the Yadava chief Shurasena, which made her Vasudeva's sister and Krishna's aunt.

questions.q: Why was the infant Pritha given to King Kuntibhoja?
options: As ransom after a border war
Because omens at her birth alarmed her father
To be educated at his famous court
Her father had vowed his firstborn child to his childless kinsman
questions.explanation: Shurasena had promised his firstborn to his childless cousin Kuntibhoja and honored that vow with Pritha.

questions.q: How did Kunti earn the boon of Durvasa's mantra?
options: By a year of patient service to the temperamental sage in her father's palace
By fasting at his forest hermitage
By memorizing the Vedas he taught her
questions.explanation: Charged with attending Durvasa during his long stay, she served him so flawlessly that he rewarded her with the mantra.

questions.q: Whose marriage to Bhima did Kunti sanction during the flight after the house of lac?
questions.explanation: Kunti approved Bhima's union with Hidimbi on set terms, and from it the giant Ghatotkacha was born.

questions.q: Before the war, Kunti sent her sons a rousing call to battle through Krishna, invoking which story?
options: The tale of Nala and Damayanti
The exhortation of Vidula to her son
questions.explanation: In the Udyoga Parva, Kunti had Krishna recite Vidula's fierce exhortation, urging her sons to fight like kshatriyas.

questions.q: When Yudhishthira objected to risking Bhima against Bakasura, how did Kunti justify her decision?
options: She cited Bhima's vast god-given strength and the debt owed their brahmin host
A dream had assured her of victory
Vyasa had sent word of the demon's weakness
She planned for all five brothers to fight together
questions.explanation: Kunti reminded him of Bhima's immense strength and argued that repaying their host's shelter was dharma.

questions.q: Arjuna's common epithet Partha derives from what?
questions.explanation: Partha means son of Pritha, honoring Kunti's original name, and Kaunteya likewise means son of Kunti.

questions.q: When her sons begged her not to leave for the forest, what reason did Kunti give?
options: A mother must always follow her husband's kin
She wished to serve her elders and win the higher worlds by austerity
She could no longer bear the memories of Karna in the city
Vyasa had summoned her to his hermitage
questions.explanation: Having seen her sons enthroned, she said her duty now was service to Dhritarashtra and Gandhari and the pursuit of penance.
